rules.name: Dashboard Layout
structural_signals: dashboard with metric cards and data tables
analytics page with charts and KPIs
rules.rationale: Dashboards require scannable metrics at a glance followed by detailed tabular data; a grid of stat cards above a data table is the most efficient layout for rapid comprehension

constraints.applies_when: page serves as an overview or summary of system state
constraints.avoid_when: fewer than 3 metrics exist or data is primarily narrative

rules.name: Application Shell
structural_signals: SaaS product with persistent nav and content area
tool with workspace and navigation panel
rules.rationale: Applications with deep feature sets need persistent navigation that does not compete with content; a sidebar provides always-visible wayfinding without consuming vertical space

constraints.applies_when: application has 4+ top-level sections with sub-pages
constraints.avoid_when: application is single-purpose or content is the primary focus

rules.name: Content-Heavy Page
rules.rationale: Long-form text demands optimal line length (50-75 characters), consistent vertical rhythm, and a type scale that establishes clear hierarchy to reduce cognitive load

constraints.applies_when: primary content is 500+ words of prose
constraints.avoid_when: content is primarily interactive or data-driven

rules.name: Split View
structural_signals: email client with list and detail pane
settings page with category list and detail
rules.rationale: Master-detail interfaces require simultaneous visibility of a collection and a selected item; a split view preserves context while showing detail without full navigation

constraints.applies_when: users need to browse and inspect items without losing list context
constraints.avoid_when: detail view requires full screen or items are independent pages

rules.name: Wizard Flow
structural_signals: configuration wizard with dependencies between steps
rules.rationale: Multi-step processes reduce cognitive load by chunking complex tasks; a stepper provides orientation and progress feedback while form validation prevents errors from compounding across steps

constraints.applies_when: process has 3+ distinct steps with logical grouping
constraints.avoid_when: all fields fit comfortably on a single screen or steps have no logical order

rules.name: Deep Hierarchy Navigation
structural_signals: enterprise app with modules, sections, and pages
rules.rationale: Deep hierarchies require expandable tree structures for exploration and breadcrumbs for orientation; hiding depth behind flat navigation creates disorientation

constraints.applies_when: information architecture exceeds 3 levels of nesting
constraints.avoid_when: hierarchy can be flattened to 2 levels or fewer

rules.name: Flat Navigation
structural_signals: simple app with few main sections
consumer app with clear primary flows
rules.rationale: Flat navigation with few items benefits from tabs or bottom navigation which provide immediate visibility of all options without hiding anything behind menus

constraints.applies_when: top-level items number 2-5 and are equally important
constraints.avoid_when: items exceed 5 or have sub-navigation needs

rules.name: Many Actions Navigation
rules.rationale: Large navigation sets overwhelm linear menus; mega menus provide structured overview while command palettes offer keyboard-driven quick access for power users

constraints.applies_when: top-level navigation items exceed 7 and cannot be consolidated
constraints.avoid_when: items can be logically grouped into fewer than 6 categories

rules.name: Mobile Navigation
rules.rationale: Mobile navigation must be thumb-reachable; bottom navigation provides persistent access to primary destinations while a drawer handles secondary navigation without consuming screen space

constraints.applies_when: primary audience uses mobile devices or viewport is under 768px
constraints.avoid_when: application is desktop-only or has fewer than 3 navigation items

rules.name: Search-Centric Navigation
structural_signals: marketplace or catalog with many items
rules.rationale: When search is the primary entry point, a prominent search input with autocomplete reduces time-to-content; command palettes add keyboard shortcuts for power users

constraints.applies_when: users typically know what they want and need to find it quickly
constraints.avoid_when: content is small enough to browse or users prefer exploratory navigation

rules.name: Long Form
rules.rationale: Forms with many fields cause abandonment when presented all at once; breaking them into steps with progress indication reduces perceived complexity and validates incrementally

constraints.applies_when: form has 10+ fields or logically distinct sections
constraints.avoid_when: all fields are required for a single atomic operation and fit on screen

rules.name: Inline Edit
structural_signals: click-to-edit fields in a detail view
rules.rationale: Inline editing eliminates context switches between view and edit modes; users stay oriented in their current task and see changes immediately in context

constraints.applies_when: individual fields are edited independently and changes are low-risk
constraints.avoid_when: fields have complex validation dependencies or edits are destructive

rules.name: Filter Form
rules.rationale: Filtering requires controls that show current selections at a glance; combining selects, checkboxes, and sliders covers categorical, boolean, and range-based filtering patterns

constraints.applies_when: dataset is filterable by 3+ dimensions
constraints.avoid_when: filtering is better served by a single search input

rules.name: Date Range Selection
rules.rationale: Date range selection needs visual calendar feedback to prevent invalid ranges; preset ranges (last 7 days, this month) accelerate common selections

constraints.applies_when: users need to select a start and end date
constraints.avoid_when: only a single date is needed or dates are predefined

rules.name: File Collection
rules.rationale: Multiple file uploads require batch progress tracking and preview to prevent duplicate uploads; drag-and-drop zones reduce friction for bulk operations

constraints.applies_when: users need to upload 2+ files in a single interaction
constraints.avoid_when: only a single file is expected or files are provided via URL

rules.name: Large Dataset
rules.rationale: Rendering 100+ rows without virtualization causes performance degradation; virtual scrolling maintains smooth interaction while pagination provides predictable data access patterns

constraints.applies_when: dataset exceeds 100 rows or total payload exceeds 1MB
constraints.avoid_when: dataset is small enough to render without performance impact

rules.name: Card Grid
rules.rationale: Visual content benefits from card-based layouts that provide consistent containers for heterogeneous content; grids maximize screen utilization and scanning efficiency

constraints.applies_when: items have visual elements (images, icons) and metadata
constraints.avoid_when: items are text-only or require detailed comparison

rules.name: Timeline Events
rules.rationale: Chronological data benefits from visual timeline representation that conveys sequence and relative timing; avatars and badges add context about actors and event types

constraints.applies_when: temporal ordering is meaningful and events span a time range
constraints.avoid_when: events lack meaningful timestamps or sequence does not matter

rules.name: Hierarchical Data
rules.rationale: Hierarchical data requires expandable/collapsible nodes to manage complexity; tree views preserve parent-child relationships visually while allowing progressive disclosure

constraints.applies_when: data has natural parent-child relationships with 2+ levels
constraints.avoid_when: hierarchy is flat or better represented as a flat filtered list

rules.name: Key-Value Display
structural_signals: profile or detail page with labeled fields
rules.rationale: Key-value pairs are best displayed as description lists which provide semantic HTML and consistent alignment between labels and values for rapid scanning

constraints.applies_when: data consists of labeled values without complex structure
constraints.avoid_when: values are complex objects or require interactive editing

rules.name: Metrics Display
rules.rationale: KPIs need immediate visual prominence with trend indicators; stat cards provide consistent containers while sparklines add temporal context without full chart complexity

constraints.applies_when: 3+ numeric metrics need to be displayed with trend context
constraints.avoid_when: a single metric is sufficient or data is non-numeric

rules.name: Async Action Feedback
structural_signals: file upload or export in progress
rules.rationale: Long-running operations need determinate progress when possible and non-blocking notifications on completion; toasts confirm completion without disrupting workflow

constraints.applies_when: operation takes more than 2 seconds to complete
constraints.avoid_when: operation completes in under 1 second or is not user-initiated

rules.name: Destructive Action Confirmation
rules.rationale: Irreversible actions require explicit confirmation to prevent accidental data loss; a confirmation dialog with a clearly labeled danger button creates a deliberate friction point

constraints.applies_when: action cannot be undone or has significant consequences
constraints.avoid_when: action is easily reversible via undo or soft-delete

rules.name: Form Error Feedback
rules.rationale: Form errors must appear adjacent to the offending field so users can correct them without scrolling or remembering which field failed; inline alerts provide this proximity

constraints.applies_when: form has validation rules that can fail
constraints.avoid_when: form has no constraints or validation is purely server-side with page reload

rules.name: Empty Collection State
structural_signals: list or table with no items
rules.rationale: Empty states are opportunities to guide users toward their first action; a clear illustration with a call-to-action reduces confusion and drives engagement

constraints.applies_when: a collection, list, or table can be empty
constraints.avoid_when: the empty state is transient and data will appear within seconds

rules.name: Loading Skeleton
rules.rationale: Skeleton screens reduce perceived load time by previewing the content structure; they outperform spinners by setting expectations about layout and reducing layout shift

constraints.applies_when: content structure is predictable and load time exceeds 300ms
constraints.avoid_when: content structure is unknown or load time is under 200ms

rules.name: System Notification
rules.rationale: System-wide messages need persistent visibility without blocking interaction; banners provide a non-dismissible strip for critical info while toasts handle transient notifications

constraints.applies_when: notification applies to the entire application or all users
constraints.avoid_when: notification is specific to a single action or user flow

rules.name: Blocking Action
rules.rationale: When user decision is required before proceeding, a modal or dialog creates a focus trap that prevents accidental bypass of the decision point

constraints.applies_when: workflow cannot continue without explicit user input
constraints.avoid_when: a reasonable default action exists or decision can be deferred

rules.name: Mobile-First Design
rules.rationale: Mobile-first design ensures the core experience works on the smallest screens first; container queries enable component-level responsiveness independent of viewport size

constraints.applies_when: 50%+ of traffic comes from mobile or mobile is the target platform
constraints.avoid_when: application is exclusively desktop (e.g., developer tools, CAD software)

rules.name: Table on Mobile
rules.rationale: Tables wider than viewport cause usability issues on mobile; converting rows to cards preserves all data while fitting narrow screens naturally

constraints.applies_when: table has 4+ columns and must work on screens under 480px
constraints.avoid_when: table has 2-3 columns that fit naturally on mobile

rules.name: Sidebar Collapse
structural_signals: navigation panel on tablet or phone
rules.rationale: Persistent sidebars consume too much horizontal space on narrow viewports; converting to a drawer preserves navigation access without sacrificing content area

constraints.applies_when: viewport width drops below 1024px and sidebar is present
constraints.avoid_when: sidebar content is critical and must remain visible at all times

rules.name: Responsive Image Gallery
rules.rationale: Image galleries need responsive grids that adapt column count to viewport width; lightbox provides full-screen viewing with pinch-to-zoom on touch devices

constraints.applies_when: displaying 4+ images that users may want to view at full size
constraints.avoid_when: images are decorative or supplementary to text content

rules.name: Color-Only Status Indicator
rules.rationale: WCAG 1.4.1 requires that color is not the sole means of conveying information; adding icons and text labels ensures colorblind users (8% of males) can perceive status

constraints.applies_when: any status, state, or category is communicated using color
constraints.avoid_when: color is purely decorative and carries no semantic meaning

rules.name: Keyboard Navigation
rules.rationale: WCAG 2.1.1 requires all functionality be operable via keyboard; custom widgets must implement WAI-ARIA keyboard interaction patterns to be accessible to screen reader and keyboard-only users

constraints.applies_when: building custom interactive components beyond native HTML elements
constraints.avoid_when: using native HTML elements that already have keyboard support

rules.name: Dynamic Content Updates
structural_signals: content that updates without page reload
rules.rationale: Screen readers do not announce DOM changes by default; ARIA live regions ensure assistive technology users are informed of dynamic updates without losing their current focus position

constraints.applies_when: content updates in response to user action or real-time events
constraints.avoid_when: updates are purely visual animations without informational content

rules.name: Form Label Accessibility
rules.rationale: WCAG 3.3.2 requires labels or instructions for user input; placeholders disappear on focus and are not reliable labels; visible persistent labels prevent user confusion and support assistive technology

constraints.applies_when: any form input exists in the interface
constraints.avoid_when: search inputs where a magnifying glass icon provides sufficient context via aria-label

rules.name: Touch Target Size
structural_signals: mobile buttons or links in close proximity
rules.rationale: WCAG 2.5.8 requires minimum 24x24px targets with sufficient spacing; Apple HIG recommends 44x44pt; undersized targets cause frustration and exclusion for users with motor impairments

constraints.applies_when: interface is used on touch devices or by users with motor impairments
constraints.avoid_when: interface is exclusively mouse-driven with precision pointing

rules.name: Motion and Animation Sensitivity
rules.rationale: WCAG 2.3.1 prohibits content that flashes more than 3 times per second; prefers-reduced-motion respects users with vestibular disorders who experience nausea or disorientation from animation

constraints.applies_when: any animation, transition, or motion effect is present
constraints.avoid_when: motion is essential to the content (e.g., demonstrating an animation tool)

rules.name: State Transition Animation
structural_signals: button hover, active, or disabled state change
rules.rationale: State transitions need subtle animation (100-200ms) with appropriate easing to provide feedback without delaying interaction; ease-out curves feel responsive while ease-in-out suits expansion

constraints.applies_when: interactive elements change visual state in response to user action
constraints.avoid_when: user has prefers-reduced-motion enabled or state change is instantaneous

rules.name: Page Transition Animation
rules.rationale: Page transitions maintain spatial orientation and reduce perceived latency; cross-fades work universally while shared-element transitions create continuity between related views

constraints.applies_when: navigating between distinct views within a single-page application
constraints.avoid_when: full page reloads occur or transitions would delay content visibility

rules.name: List Reorder Animation
structural_signals: filtered list with items appearing and disappearing
rules.rationale: Animating list changes helps users track which items moved where; staggered animations create visual flow while FLIP technique ensures performant layout transitions

constraints.applies_when: list items change position, appear, or disappear dynamically
constraints.avoid_when: list has 50+ items changing simultaneously or user prefers reduced motion

rules.name: Loading Animation
rules.rationale: Loading animations reduce perceived wait time and assure users that the system is working; skeleton shimmer is preferred over spinners as it previews content structure

constraints.applies_when: content load takes 300ms or longer
constraints.avoid_when: content loads instantly or a progress bar with percentage is more appropriate

rules.name: Brand Color Application
rules.rationale: The 60-30-10 rule (60% dominant, 30% secondary, 10% accent) creates visual balance; semantic color tokens (primary, secondary, accent) decouple brand from function for maintainability

constraints.applies_when: brand identity needs to be expressed through the interface
constraints.avoid_when: interface is a white-label product where brand colors are injected at runtime

rules.name: Dark Theme Support
rules.rationale: Dark themes reduce eye strain in low-light environments and save battery on OLED screens; semantic tokens ensure colors adapt correctly without hard-coded values breaking in dark mode

constraints.applies_when: users request dark mode or application is used in variable lighting
constraints.avoid_when: brand guidelines strictly require light-only presentation

rules.name: Content Typography
rules.rationale: Perfect fourth (1.333) type scale creates clear hierarchy for content-heavy pages; vertical rhythm with consistent line-height multiples creates visual harmony that reduces reading fatigue

constraints.applies_when: primary content is long-form text meant to be read
constraints.avoid_when: content is primarily data, controls, or interactive elements

rules.name: UI Typography
structural_signals: form labels, buttons, and navigation text
rules.rationale: Major third (1.25) type scale provides sufficient hierarchy without excessive size jumps in UI contexts; tighter scales keep interfaces compact while maintaining readability

constraints.applies_when: typography serves UI elements rather than content consumption
constraints.avoid_when: interface includes substantial reading content mixed with controls

rules.name: Contextual Menu
structural_signals: item-specific actions in a list or grid
overflow actions that do not fit in visible UI
rules.rationale: Contextual menus surface actions relevant to a specific item or selection without cluttering the persistent UI; they follow platform conventions users already understand

constraints.applies_when: items have 3+ secondary actions that are not used frequently enough for dedicated buttons
constraints.avoid_when: primary actions should be immediately visible without interaction

rules.name: Notification Center
rules.rationale: When notifications accumulate, a centralized notification center prevents toast fatigue and gives users control over when they review updates; badge counters provide awareness without interruption

constraints.applies_when: application generates 5+ notifications per session from different sources
constraints.avoid_when: notifications are rare or all require immediate attention

rules.name: Error Boundary Display
structural_signals: API failure with no fallback data
rules.rationale: Graceful error boundaries prevent cascading failures from crashing the entire application; a fallback UI with retry option gives users agency to recover without full page reload

constraints.applies_when: application uses component-based architecture with potential isolated failures
constraints.avoid_when: error affects the entire application and requires a full error page

rules.name: Data Visualization Selection
structural_signals: trends over time or comparisons between categories
rules.rationale: Choosing the right chart type is critical: line charts for trends, bar charts for comparison, pie/donut for composition, scatter for correlation; mismatched chart types mislead users

constraints.applies_when: numeric data benefits from visual pattern recognition over tabular reading
constraints.avoid_when: exact values matter more than patterns or dataset is too small to visualize meaningfully

rules.name: Drag and Drop Interaction
rules.rationale: Drag and drop provides intuitive spatial manipulation but must include keyboard alternatives for accessibility; visible drag handles indicate draggable elements and drop zones confirm valid targets

constraints.applies_when: spatial arrangement or ordering is a primary user task
constraints.avoid_when: items have a natural sort order or reordering is an infrequent action

rules.name: Infinite Scroll vs Pagination
rules.rationale: Infinite scroll suits content consumption where users browse without a specific target; a load-more button hybrid gives users control while scroll position restoration prevents frustration on back-navigation

constraints.applies_when: content is consumed sequentially and items are relatively uniform
constraints.avoid_when: users need to reach the footer, compare specific pages, or share positions via URL

rules.name: Focus Visibility
rules.rationale: WCAG 2.4.7 requires visible focus indicators for keyboard navigation; :focus-visible distinguishes keyboard from mouse focus, preventing visual noise for mouse users while ensuring keyboard accessibility

constraints.applies_when: any interactive element exists in the interface
constraints.avoid_when: never — focus visibility should always be implemented

rules.name: Responsive Typography
structural_signals: text that must scale across breakpoints
headings that are too large on mobile
body text that is too small on desktop
rules.rationale: Fluid typography using CSS clamp() scales smoothly between minimum and maximum sizes without breakpoint jumps; this eliminates text reflow at breakpoints and maintains readability across all viewports

constraints.applies_when: text must be readable from 320px to 1920px+ viewport widths
constraints.avoid_when: fixed typography sizes are required by brand guidelines

rules.name: Color Contrast Compliance
structural_signals: light gray text on white background
overlapping transparency or gradients with text
rules.rationale: WCAG 1.4.3 requires 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text and 3:1 for large text; insufficient contrast affects users with low vision, representing 15% of the global population

constraints.applies_when: any text is displayed against a background color
constraints.avoid_when: text is purely decorative and not intended to be read

rules.name: Spacing System
rules.rationale: A 4px base spacing scale (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64) provides granular control while maintaining visual consistency; spacing tokens make refactoring effortless and enforce design system constraints

constraints.applies_when: building or maintaining a design system with multiple components
constraints.avoid_when: one-off prototypes where spacing consistency is not a priority

rules.name: Toast vs Alert Selection
rules.rationale: Toasts are appropriate for transient confirmations that do not require user action; they auto-dismiss after 5-8 seconds and do not block workflow, unlike alerts which persist until dismissed

constraints.applies_when: feedback is confirmatory and does not require user action
constraints.avoid_when: message contains critical information, errors, or required actions

rules.name: Responsive Grid System
structural_signals: card or content grid needing responsive columns
rules.rationale: CSS Grid with auto-fill and minmax creates intrinsically responsive layouts without media queries; container queries further enable components to adapt based on their own space rather than viewport

constraints.applies_when: layout requires multi-column arrangement that adapts to available space
constraints.avoid_when: layout is single-column or has fixed requirements at all sizes

rules.name: Modal Alternatives Assessment
structural_signals: overuse of modal dialogs in flow
modal within modal or nested modals
rules.rationale: Modal overuse causes dialog fatigue and blocks underlying content; alternatives like inline expansion, drawers, or dedicated pages maintain context and reduce cognitive interruption

constraints.applies_when: content in modal could be shown inline or on its own page
constraints.avoid_when: user decision is truly required before proceeding

rules.name: Semantic Color Tokens
structural_signals: inconsistent use of colors for similar purposes
rules.rationale: Semantic tokens (color-success, color-danger, color-text-primary) decouple intent from specific color values, enabling theming, dark mode, and accessibility high-contrast mode without component changes

constraints.applies_when: building a design system or application supporting multiple themes
constraints.avoid_when: single-use prototype with no theming requirements

rules.name: Animation Performance
structural_signals: animating layout properties like width or height
rules.rationale: Only transform and opacity trigger GPU compositing without layout recalculation; animating width, height, margin, or padding forces expensive relayout on every frame, causing jank on 60fps displays

constraints.applies_when: any CSS or JavaScript animation is implemented
constraints.avoid_when: animation is a single-frame transition with no visible interpolation

rules.name: Skip Navigation Link
structural_signals: page with repeated header and navigation
application with toolbar before main content
repeated content blocks before primary content
rules.rationale: WCAG 2.4.1 requires a mechanism to bypass repeated blocks; skip navigation links let keyboard and screen reader users jump directly to main content without tabbing through the entire header on every page

constraints.applies_when: page has repeated navigation or header content before main content
constraints.avoid_when: single-page application with no repeated blocks

wcag_references: WCAG 2.3.1 - Three Flashes or Below Threshold
metadata.description: Structural signal to design pattern matching rules for Theia, the Design Titan. Maps UI structural signals to recommended component patterns, design system primitives, and accessibility standards.
